hypothesis: Incorporating a DFT+U correction specifically calibrated for 3d transition metal MOFs as an intermediate fidelity level in a multi-fidelity ∆-learning cascade (GFN1-xTB → DFT+U → HSE06) will yield greater band gap prediction accuracy for transition-metal-containing MOFs than the two-level GFN1-xTB/HSE06 ∆-learning scheme, because the explicit treatment of strong d-electron correlation at the DFT+U level will reduce the systematic underestimation of band gaps identified for Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, and Ni MOFs, providing a more informative correction signal that the XGBoost model can learn from with fewer high-fidelity HSE06 training points.
